headlines.title: US Carries out 10th Night of Strikes After Service Members Killed
headlines.description: United States Central Command released video of its 10th night of consecutive strikes flying deep into Iran, targeting command centers, launch sites and air defenses. President Donald Trump says Iran “will pay” anytime they kill an American servicemember. Meanwhile, the State Department issued a rare global travel warning to Americans abroad after Iran sent another barrage of missiles into Jordan. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: U.S. military identifies soldiers killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
headlines.description: The Pentagon identified the soldiers as 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales of Texas.

headlines.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy fires his commander in chief after protests and major rift
headlines.description: The firing caps a major crisis for Volodymyr Zelenskyy after Ukrainians took to the streets to call for Oleksandr Syrskyi’s resignation.

headlines.title: RFK Jr. says cyclosporiasis outbreak is ‘under control’ amid investigation into its origins
headlines.description: Mexico said its own investigation hasn’t found evidence linking the outbreak to the Taylor Farms facility in central Mexico.

headlines.title: Child actor Kaylee Hottle killed in car crash
headlines.description: The 18-year-old “Godzilla vs. Kong” star was killed when the car she was riding in veered off the road and hit a culvert just before 3 a.m. Tuesday.

headlines.title: We want to hear how Medicaid work requirements are affecting you
headlines.description: If you have lost your health coverage or are at risk of losing it, tell us your story.

headlines.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
headlines.description: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: U.S. attorney who served for 54 minutes sues over firing by Trump
headlines.description: The Trump administration has fired several U.S. attorneys appointed by federal judges. Roger Rogoff is the first to sue over being removed.

headlines.title: RFK Jr. says cyclosporiasis outbreak is ‘under control’ amid investigation into its origins
headlines.description: Mexico said its own investigation hasn’t found evidence linking the outbreak to the Taylor Farms facility in central Mexico.

headlines.title: What to watch for in Arizona tonight: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, Ben Kamisar previews the key contests to keep an eye on as primary results roll in across Arizona. Plus, our White House team digs into a critical new phase of the Iran war for President Donald Trump.

headlines.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy fires his commander in chief after protests and major rift
headlines.description: The firing caps a major crisis for Volodymyr Zelenskyy after Ukrainians took to the streets to call for Oleksandr Syrskyi’s resignation.

headlines.title: 6,600 noncitizens were registered to vote in N.J. because of a ‘software error,’ governor says
headlines.description: Democratic Gov. Mikie Sherrill said a review found that around 400 noncitizens cast ballots in elections from 2023 to 2024. The DOJ said it’s investigating the matter.
